channel.title: News on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in News on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Tuesday Brief: Opposition Leader Coming Monday, Police Academy Scandal, and Georgetown Is STILL a Dumpster Fire
item.description:
Your 5-minute Guyanese news circus — now with 100% more political drama ☕🇬🇾

🗳️ OPPOSITION LEADER: FINALLY! MAYBE! MONDAY!

What Happened: Speaker Manzoor Nadir announced he will “reluctantly” convene a meeting of opposition MPs on Monday, January 26, at 10 AM to elect the Leader of the Opposition.

The Key Word: “Reluctantly.”

Translation: “Fine! FINE! I’ll do it! Stop calling me!”

The Math:

  • General elections: October 2025
  • Months without Opposition Leader: 3+
  • Speaker’s level of enthusiasm: “Reluctant”

Who’s Likely to Win: Azruddin Mohamed of WIN, which holds 16 of 29 opposition seats. Yes, the same Azruddin Mohamed who is under US sanctions. Democracy is fun!

item.title: Tuesday Brief: Cash Grants Are So Last Year, Georgetown Is a Dumpster Fire, and Teachers Want Answers
item.description:
Your 5-minute Guyanese news circus — now with 100% less cash grants ☕🇬🇾

💸 CASH GRANTS: “NOT SUSTAINABLE” — PRESIDENT

What Happened: President Ali declared that future cash grants are “not sustainable,” warning that “some people would use this as a political opportunity.”

The Backstory: Just one year ago, the government was “committed to making future cash grants.” What a difference 365 days makes!

The Math:

  • 2025: “We are committed to future cash grants!”
  • 2026: “Cash grants are not sustainable.”
  • Also 2026: Oil production approaching 1 million barrels per day.

Dem Boys Seh: “When election coming, cash grant sustainable. When election done, cash grant unsustainable. Is like magic — de money just disappear!”

item.title: Sunday's Guyana Brief - Hotels Multiply, Health Goes Digital, and Cricket Women Keep Winning
item.description: Another US$18M hotel opens, your medical records go online, President Ali says cash grants aren’t sustainable (after promising one), Women’s cricket squad stays undefeated, and Fruta Conquerors lose their president after 11 months.

item.title: Saturday's Guyana Brief: Toy Guns Guarding Your Groceries, Solar Power Rising, and Guyana Women Battle Jamaica Tonight!
item.description: Security guards caught with toy guns at supermarkets, US$8.14M solar farm lights up Charity, GBTI launches private banking for the rich, and Guyana Women seek redemption against Jamaica under the lights!

item.title: Friday's Guyana Brief - Oil Wells Racing to Empty, Speaker Races to India
item.description:
Good morning, Guyana! ☕

Welcome to Friday, where our oil fields are being liquidated faster than a closing-down sale, our Speaker fled to India while Parliament remains closed, and Exxon’s 75% “expense deduction” makes Nigerian email scams look amateur.

Today’s menu: The Great Oil Heist exposed (75% goes to “costs”), Speaker escapes to India during constitutional crisis, French Ambassador politely suggests democracy might be nice, Mashramani launches because at least we can party, and President Ali promises no Venezuela deals (this time he means it, promise).

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Wednesday Brief: Guyanese Takes IICA Helm, Census Controversy, and Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: Muhammad Ibrahim becomes IICA Director-General, census data sparks housing debate, cybercrime law faces criticism, and AG slams courts for ignoring legislation.

item.title: Tuesday's Guyana Brief: Apps, Laws, and Courtroom Drama
item.description: Attorney General scolds magistrates for ignoring laws, government promises 87 new apps, and President Ali discovers swamps can become highways (who knew?). Welcome to Tuesday in paradise!

item.title: 🔍 [CRITICAL] Monday Brief: Crime Stats, Search & Rescue Upgrade, and Traffic Chaos
item.description: Murders up 11% despite overall crime drop, new $123M search and rescue system launched, over 3,100 traffic offences in one week, and financial system reforms promised.

item.title: Monday's Guyana Brief: The Week Starts With a Yelling
item.description:
Good morning, Guyana! ☕

Welcome to Monday, where last week’s problems are this week’s problems with a fresh coat of optimistic denial.

Nothing got fixed over the weekend. Shocked? You shouldn’t be.

Today’s menu: GuySuCo gets scolded (again), The Mohameds perfect the art of not leaving, crime stats achieve Olympic-level creativity, and we learn why complaining about bribery is somehow worse than actual bribery. Just another week in paradise!

📊 TODAY’S NUMBERS

Mohamed Appeals: 47 (their lawyer’s yacht fund thanks you)
